items.title: Modern Warfare 4: Why CoD fans remain deeply skeptical
items.desc: Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 has been officially unveiled – and on paper, the shooter delivers exactly what many fans want: a war-focused Korea setting, a dark campaign, the return of DMZ and reworked gunplay. Nevertheless, many fans remain wary.

items.title: US Treasury Chief Warns Oman On Sanctions Day After Trump Threatens To 'Blow Them Up'
items.desc: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent on May 28 threatened to target longtime Gulf ally Oman with sanctions if it helps Iran impose a tolling system in the strategic Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Netanyahu orders Israeli military to expand Gaza occupation to 70%
items.desc: Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu has given directives for the Israeli military to expand occupation of Gaza to 70 percent for now, as his cabinet minister has confirmed the regime's plan for ethnic cleansing in the strip.

items.desc: Exercising the tactical defence of Malaysia and Singapore is an enduring task with a lot of training benefits for the New Zealand Defence Force (NZDF), says Squadron Leader Pete Jackson.

items.title: Romania Accuses Russia Of 'Serious Escalation' After Drone Hits Apartment Building
items.desc: Romania has accused Russia of a "serious and irresponsible escalation" after a drone hit a high-rise building inside Romanian territory near the border with Ukraine.

items.title: Legislature approves NT$8.8 billion 2026 defense procurement budget
items.desc: Taiwan's Legislature on Friday approved a 2026 special defense procurement budget of more than NT$8.81 billion (US$258.7 million) to fund purchases of five U.S. weapons systems.
